page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2ne.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz with an x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-threaded and multi-threaded capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by bottom 25% stress-ng single-core results and poor single-threaded gzip compression. However, the dedicated 16-core architecture delivers average multi-core performance in stress-ng and bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks remains in the average tier, supporting steady data throughput.

Given its resource profile, the ecs.sn2ne.4xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation and balanced memory-to-vCPU ratios without requiring high-speed local storage. It is well-suited for static web serving, Redis caching, database operations, and multi-threaded processing tasks. It is less optimal for single-threaded applications or medium-to-large language model inference due to lower single-core efficiency.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2ne.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 physical cores at 2.5 GHz and 64.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Benchmark data shows weak single-core performance but average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. This resource profile makes it qualitatively cost-effective for balanced, multi-threaded workloads such as static web serving, Redis caching, and database operations, while it remains less suited for single-threaded tasks or intensive machine learning inference.
